title: Clinical Tissue Indices Explained
text: Tissue oxygenation assessment via HSI relies distinct absorption spectra oxyhemoglobin (HbO2) deoxyhemoglobin (Hb).
code: hb_data <- hs_chromophore_data(c("HbO2", "Hb"), wavelength_range = c(430, 910)) library(ggplot2) ggplot(hb_data, aes(x = wavelength)) +   geom_line(aes(y = HbO2, color = "HbO2"), linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = Hb, color = "Hb"), linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(HbO2 = "#E41A1C", Hb = "#377EB8")) +   labs(x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Extinction Coefficient",        title = "Hemoglobin Absorption Spectra", color = "") +   theme_hsi()

title: Clinical Tissue Indices Explained
text: StO2 estimates superficial tissue oxygenation (~1 mm depth) comparing reflectance visible Hb absorption region (500-650 nm) NIR region (700-815 nm).
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() sto2 <- hs_sto2(cube) hs_plot_index(sto2, title = "StO2 (%)", palette = "sto2")

title: Clinical Tissue Indices Explained
text: NPI probes deeper tissue layers (4-6 mm) using NIR wavelengths (655-910 nm).

title: Clinical Tissue Indices Explained
text: THI estimates relative hemoglobin concentration Q-band absorption depth (530-590 nm) relative reference region (785-825 nm).

title: Clinical Tissue Indices Explained
text: Create two-band ratio index:
code: ndi <- hs_ndi(cube, band1 = 540, band2 = 660) hs_plot_index(ndi, title = "NDI (540/660)", range = c(-1, 1))

title: Clinical Tissue Indices Explained
text: TIVITA-style 5-panel display combines RGB indices:

title: Compartment Syndrome HSI Assessment
text: Acute compartment syndrome (ACS) surgical emergency increased pressure within closed muscle compartment compromises tissue perfusion. Traditional diagnosis relies clinical assessment invasive intra- compartmental pressure (ICP) measurement. Hyperspectral imaging offers non-invasive alternative quantifying tissue oxygenation skin surface, enabling spatial mapping perfusion compromise.

title: Compartment Syndrome HSI Assessment
text: simulate tissue scene region reduced oxygenation representing compartment syndrome:
code: cube <- hs_simulate_cube(rows = 50, cols = 50, n_regions = 4,                           sto2_range = c(0.15, 0.9), seed = 123)

title: Compartment Syndrome HSI Assessment
text: Regions StO2 40% may indicate critical ischemia.
code: sto2 <- hs_sto2(cube) hs_plot_index(sto2, title = "StO2 - Extremity (%)", palette = "sto2")

title: Compartment Syndrome HSI Assessment
code: npi <- hs_npi(cube) hs_plot_index(npi, title = "NPI - Deep Perfusion (%)", palette = "perfusion")

title: Compartment Syndrome HSI Assessment
text: affected compartment shows reduced reflectance visible range (increased Hb absorption) altered spectral shape, consistent venous congestion tissue deoxygenation.
code: roi_affected <- hs_roi_rect(cube, x_range = c(30, 50), y_range = c(30, 50)) roi_normal <- hs_roi_rect(cube, x_range = c(1, 20), y_range = c(1, 20))  stats_aff <- hs_roi_stats(cube, roi_affected) stats_norm <- hs_roi_stats(cube, roi_normal)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() +   geom_line(data = stats_norm,             aes(x = wavelength, y = mean, color = "Normal"), linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(data = stats_aff,             aes(x = wavelength, y = mean, color = "Affected"), linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(Normal = "#2E86AB", Affected = "#E41A1C")) +   labs(x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Reflectance",        title = "Spectral Comparison: Normal vs. Affected Tissue", color = "") +   theme_hsi()

title: Cubert Camera Data Workflow
text: vignette demonstrates complete workflow processing hyperspectral data Cubert Ultris X MR camera. examples use synthetic data, code translates directly real camera acquisitions.

title: Cubert Camera Data Workflow
text: Cubert Ultris X MR snapshot mosaic hyperspectral camera: Wavelength range: 430-910 nm Spectral bands: 61 Spectral sampling: 8 nm FWHM: ~25 nm Spatial resolution: 1000 x 1000 pixels Frame rate: 17 Hz

title: Cubert Camera Data Workflow
text: exporting CUVIS Export ENVI format: vignette use synthetic data:
code: # Read the ENVI header + binary pair cube <- hs_read_envi("path/to/measurement.hdr")  # Or use the auto-detect reader cube <- hs_read_cube("path/to/measurement.hdr") cube <- hs_simulate_cube(rows = 30, cols = 30, seed = 42) print(cube) #>  #> ── hsi_cube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Dimensions: 30 rows x 30 cols x 61 bands #> Wavelengths: 430-910 nm (61 bands) #> FWHM: 25 nm (mean) #> Mask: 900/900 valid pixels (100%) #> Data range: [0.0198, 0.7306] #> Metadata: camera, processing_mode, acquisition_time, region_map, #> sto2_ground_truth, seed

title: Cubert Camera Data Workflow
text: Apply dark current subtraction white reference normalization:
code: # Simulate dark and white references dark_data <- array(0.02, dim(cube$data)) white_data <- array(0.95, dim(cube$data)) dark <- hsi_cube(dark_data, cube$wavelengths) white <- hsi_cube(white_data, cube$wavelengths)  # Calibrate to reflectance calibrated <- hs_calibrate(cube, dark, white) range(calibrated$data) #> [1] 0.000000 0.764044

title: Cubert Camera Data Workflow
text: Apply spectral smoothing reduce noise:
code: smoothed <- hs_smooth(calibrated, window = 5, poly = 2) hs_plot_spectra(smoothed, pixels = "mean", show_sd = TRUE)

title: Cubert Camera Data Workflow
code: # Save processed cube hs_write_envi(smoothed, "path/to/processed_cube")  # Export index map as PNG sto2 <- hs_sto2(smoothed) hs_export_png(sto2, "path/to/sto2_map.png")

title: Getting Started with hyperspectR
code: library(hyperspectR) #> hyperspectR v0.1.0 - Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications

title: Getting Started with hyperspectR
text: hyperspectR provides synthetic data generators testing demonstration. hs_example_cube() creates 30x30 pixel tissue scene 61 spectral bands spanning 430-910 nm: Check dimensions: summary provides per-band statistics:
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() print(cube) #>  #> ── hsi_cube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Dimensions: 30 rows x 30 cols x 61 bands #> Wavelengths: 430-910 nm (61 bands) #> FWHM: 25 nm (mean) #> Mask: 900/900 valid pixels (100%) #> Data range: [0.0198, 0.7306] #> Metadata: camera, processing_mode, acquisition_time, region_map, #> sto2_ground_truth, seed dim(cube) #> [1] 30 30 61 s <- summary(cube) s$wavelength_range #> [1] 430 910 s$data_range #> [1] 0.01982067 0.73056093

title: Getting Started with hyperspectR
text: Portions package prepared assistance large language model tooling narrowly defined, non-authorial tasks: copyediting, prose smoothing, Markdown/LaTeX formatting, scaffolding boilerplate files (CI configs, build scripts), code refactoring. tools used Chat AI, LLM service KISSKI (GWDG), self-hosted Mistral Small (24B, Apache-2.0) run locally via Ollama ollamar R package — local inference , data sent third parties self-hosted model. scientific claims, methodological choices, analyses, interpretations, conclusions author’s . LLM-generated text incorporated without review revision, every reference verified DOI, arXiv ID, ISBN.

title: Intraoperative Oxygenation Mapping
text: vignette demonstrates use hyperspectR intraoperative tissue oxygenation assessment. simulate tissue scene walk complete analysis pipeline raw data publication-quality figures.

title: Intraoperative Oxygenation Mapping
code: cube <- hs_simulate_cube(rows = 50, cols = 50, n_regions = 4,                           sto2_range = c(0.2, 0.95), seed = 42) print(cube) #>  #> ── hsi_cube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Dimensions: 50 rows x 50 cols x 61 bands #> Wavelengths: 430-910 nm (61 bands) #> FWHM: 25 nm (mean) #> Mask: 2500/2500 valid pixels (100%) #> Data range: [0.0163, 0.734] #> Metadata: camera, processing_mode, acquisition_time, region_map, #> sto2_ground_truth, seed

title: Intraoperative Oxygenation Mapping
code: sto2_ratio <- hs_sto2(smoothed, method = "ratio") hs_plot_index(sto2_ratio, title = "StO2 - Band Ratio (%)", palette = "sto2")

title: Intraoperative Oxygenation Mapping
code: fit <- hs_beer_lambert(smoothed) hs_plot_index(fit$sto2, title = "StO2 - Beer-Lambert (%)", palette = "sto2")

title: Intraoperative Oxygenation Mapping
code: # Define ROIs for different surgical field regions roi_healthy <- hs_roi_rect(smoothed, x_range = c(1, 20), y_range = c(1, 20)) roi_ischemic <- hs_roi_rect(smoothed, x_range = c(30, 50), y_range = c(1, 20))  stats_healthy <- hs_roi_stats(smoothed, roi_healthy) stats_ischemic <- hs_roi_stats(smoothed, roi_ischemic)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() +   geom_ribbon(data = stats_healthy,               aes(x = wavelength, ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd),               fill = "#2E86AB", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(data = stats_healthy,             aes(x = wavelength, y = mean, color = "Region 1"), linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_ribbon(data = stats_ischemic,               aes(x = wavelength, ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd),               fill = "#E41A1C", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(data = stats_ischemic,             aes(x = wavelength, y = mean, color = "Region 2"), linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("Region 1" = "#2E86AB", "Region 2" = "#E41A1C")) +   labs(x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Reflectance",        title = "ROI Spectral Comparison", color = "") +   theme_hsi()

title: Intraoperative Oxygenation Mapping
code: pca <- hs_pca(smoothed, n_components = 3) cat("Variance explained:", round(pca$variance_explained * 100, 1), "%\n") #> Variance explained: 86.7 1.2 1.1 %

what: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications
title: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications
text: hyperspectR provides complete R pipeline biomedical hyperspectral imaging analysis – raw camera data clinical tissue oxygenation maps.

title: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications
Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications
code: library(hyperspectR)  # Load example cube (synthetic 30x30 tissue scene, 61 bands, 430-910 nm) cube <- hs_example_cube() print(cube)  # Plot RGB composite autoplot(cube, type = "rgb")  # Compute tissue oxygenation sto2 <- hs_sto2(cube) hs_plot_index(sto2, title = "StO2 (%)", palette = "sto2")  # Clinical 5-panel display (TIVITA-style) hs_plot_clinical(cube)  # Launch interactive explorer hs_run_app(cube)

title: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications
text: /O: Read ENVI, multi-channel TIFF, Cubert .cu3s files Calibration: Dark correction, white reference normalization, bad pixel repair Preprocessing: Savitzky-Golay smoothing, SNV, MSC, spectral derivatives Biomedical indices: StO2, NPI, THI, TWI, custom normalized difference indices Analysis: PCA, MNF, SAM classification, SVM/RF pixel classification, Beer-Lambert unmixing Visualization: ggplot2-based spectral plots, clinical panel displays, interactive Shiny app Clinical focus: Intraoperative oxygenation mapping, compartment syndrome assessment

title: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications
text: Portions package prepared assistance large language model tooling narrowly defined, non-authorial tasks: copyediting, prose smoothing, Markdown/LaTeX formatting, scaffolding boilerplate files (CI configs, build scripts), code refactoring. tools used Chat AI, LLM service KISSKI (GWDG), self-hosted Mistral Small (24B, Apache-2.0) run locally via Ollama ollamar R package — local inference , data sent third parties self-hosted model. scientific claims, methodological choices, analyses, interpretations, conclusions author’s . title: Convert hsi_cube to Data Frame — as.data.frame.hsi_cube
text: x hsi_cube object. ... Additional arguments (ignored). long Logical. TRUE, returns long format columns x, y, wavelength, value. FALSE, returns wide format columns x, y, one column per band. Default FALSE.

text: x hsi_cube object. ... Additional arguments (ignored). long Logical. TRUE (default), returns long format. See .data.frame.hsi_cube() details. .name_repair Name repair strategy (passed tibble::as_tibble()).

title: Convert hsi_cube to Tibble — as_tibble.hsi_cube
text: tibble::tibble.

title: Plot an hsi_cube Object — autoplot.hsi_cube
text: Creates ggplot2 visualization hyperspectral cube. Supports RGB composite, single-band images, spectral profile plots.

title: Plot an hsi_cube Object — autoplot.hsi_cube
code: # S3 method for class 'hsi_cube' autoplot(   object,   type = c("rgb", "band", "spectra"),   band = NULL,   wavelength = NULL,   r = NULL,   g = NULL,   b = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot an hsi_cube Object — autoplot.hsi_cube
text: object hsi_cube object. type Character. Plot type: "rgb" (default), "band", "spectra". band Integer. Band index single-band display. Used type = "band". wavelength Numeric. Wavelength (nm) single-band display. Alternative band. r, g, b Numeric. Wavelengths RGB channels. Defaults: 640, 550, 460. ... Additional arguments passed internal plot functions.

title: Plot an hsi_cube Object — autoplot.hsi_cube
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

text: Get Dimensions hsi_cube

title: Get Dimensions of an hsi_cube — dim.hsi_cube
Get Dimensions of an hsi_cube — dim.hsi_cube
text: x hsi_cube object.

title: Get Dimensions of an hsi_cube — dim.hsi_cube
text: Integer vector c(rows, cols, bands).

title: Get Dimensions of an hsi_cube — dim.hsi_cube
Convert Reflectance to Absorbance — hs_absorbance
text: Computes (lambda) = -log10(R(lambda)). Values R zero clamped floor log transformation.

title: Convert Reflectance to Absorbance — hs_absorbance
text: cube hsi_cube object reflectance values. floor Numeric. Minimum reflectance value prevent log(0). Default 1e-6.

title: Convert Reflectance to Absorbance — hs_absorbance
text: hsi_cube object absorbance values.

title: Beer-Lambert Chromophore Fitting — hs_beer_lambert
text: Estimates pixel-wise concentrations tissue chromophores fitting absorbance spectra published extinction coefficient spectra using NNLS.

title: Beer-Lambert Chromophore Fitting — hs_beer_lambert
code: hs_beer_lambert(   cube,   chromophores = c("HbO2", "Hb"),   wavelength_range = c(500, 600) )

title: Beer-Lambert Chromophore Fitting — hs_beer_lambert
text: cube hsi_cube object. reflectance, automatically converted absorbance internally. chromophores Character vector. Default c("HbO2", "Hb"). wavelength_range Numeric vector length 2. Fitting range. Default c(500, 600) (Hb Q-band region best contrast).

title: Beer-Lambert Chromophore Fitting — hs_beer_lambert
text: list class "hsi_chromophore_fit": concentrations Named list matrices (one per chromophore). sto2 Matrix oxygen saturation = HbO2 / (HbO2 + Hb) * 100. total_hb Matrix total hemoglobin = HbO2 + Hb. rmse Matrix fit residuals.

title: Beer-Lambert Chromophore Fitting — hs_beer_lambert
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() fit <- hs_beer_lambert(cube) range(fit$sto2, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 42.1452 73.0292

what: Calibrate Raw HSI Data to Reflectance — hs_calibrate
title: Calibrate Raw HSI Data to Reflectance — hs_calibrate
text: Applies dark current subtraction white reference normalization: R(x,y,lambda) = (raw - dark) / (white - dark). Values clamped [0, 1] unless clamp = FALSE.

title: Calibrate Raw HSI Data to Reflectance — hs_calibrate
Calibrate Raw HSI Data to Reflectance — hs_calibrate
text: cube hsi_cube object raw data. dark hsi_cube object 3D array representing dark reference (lens cap measurement). white hsi_cube object 3D array representing white reference (Spectralon panel measurement). clamp Logical. Clamp output [0, 1]. Default TRUE.

title: Calibrate Raw HSI Data to Reflectance — hs_calibrate
text: hsi_cube object reflectance values.

title: Calibrate Raw HSI Data to Reflectance — hs_calibrate
code: cube <- hs_simulate_cube(rows = 10, cols = 10, noise_sd = 0) dark <- hsi_cube(array(0.01, dim(cube$data)), cube$wavelengths) white <- hsi_cube(array(0.95, dim(cube$data)), cube$wavelengths) cal <- hs_calibrate(cube, dark, white) range(cal$data) #> [1] 0.04255319 0.73404255

what: Get Chromophore Extinction Coefficient Spectra — hs_chromophore_data
title: Get Chromophore Extinction Coefficient Spectra — hs_chromophore_data
text: Returns published molar extinction coefficient spectra common tissue chromophores. Data Prahl (Oregon Medical Laser Center) Jacques (2013).

title: Get Chromophore Extinction Coefficient Spectra — hs_chromophore_data
Get Chromophore Extinction Coefficient Spectra — hs_chromophore_data
text: chromophore Character vector. One : "HbO2" (oxyhemoglobin), "Hb" (deoxyhemoglobin), "water", "melanin", "metHb" (methemoglobin). Default c("HbO2", "Hb"). wavelength_range Numeric vector length 2. Wavelength range nm. Default c(400, 1000).

title: Get Chromophore Extinction Coefficient Spectra — hs_chromophore_data
text: tibble::tibble columns wavelength (nm) one column per requested chromophore (extinction coefficient cm^-1 / (mol/L)).

title: Get Chromophore Extinction Coefficient Spectra — hs_chromophore_data
Random Forest Pixel Classification — hs_classify_rf
text: Trains random forest labeled pixels classifies pixels. Requires ranger package.

title: Random Forest Pixel Classification — hs_classify_rf
code: hs_classify_rf(   cube,   training_labels,   training_mask = NULL,   num.trees = 500L,   mtry = NULL,   ... )

title: Random Forest Pixel Classification — hs_classify_rf
text: cube hsi_cube object. training_labels Character factor matrix class labels. NA unlabeled pixels. training_mask Logical matrix. Default NULL. num.trees Integer. Number trees. Default 500. mtry Integer. Variables per split. Default NULL (auto). ... Additional arguments passed ranger::ranger().

title: Random Forest Pixel Classification — hs_classify_rf
text: list class "hsi_classification" containing class_map.

title: Random Forest Pixel Classification — hs_classify_rf
code: # \donttest{ # Requires ranger package cube <- hs_example_cube() labels <- matrix(NA_character_, 30, 30) labels[1:10, 1:10] <- "class_a" labels[20:30, 20:30] <- "class_b" if (requireNamespace("ranger", quietly = TRUE)) {   result <- hs_classify_rf(cube, labels) } # }

title: SVM Pixel Classification — hs_classify_svm
text: Trains Support Vector Machine labeled pixels classifies pixels. Requires e1071 package.

title: SVM Pixel Classification — hs_classify_svm
code: hs_classify_svm(   cube,   training_labels,   training_mask = NULL,   kernel = "radial",   cost = 10,   gamma = NULL,   ... )

title: SVM Pixel Classification — hs_classify_svm
text: cube hsi_cube object. training_labels Character factor matrix (spatial dims cube) class labels. NA unlabeled pixels. training_mask Logical matrix. Alternative NA labels. Default NULL. kernel Character. SVM kernel: "radial" (default), "linear", "polynomial". cost Numeric. Cost parameter. Default 10. gamma Numeric. Gamma parameter. Default NULL (auto). ... Additional arguments passed e1071::svm().

title: SVM Pixel Classification — hs_classify_svm
text: list class "hsi_classification" containing class_map.

title: SVM Pixel Classification — hs_classify_svm
code: # \donttest{ # Requires e1071 package cube <- hs_example_cube() labels <- matrix(NA_character_, 30, 30) labels[1:10, 1:10] <- "class_a" labels[20:30, 20:30] <- "class_b" if (requireNamespace("e1071", quietly = TRUE)) {   result <- hs_classify_svm(cube, labels) } # }

Compute All Available Clinical Indices — hs_clinical_indices
text: cube hsi_cube object reflectance data.

title: Compute All Available Clinical Indices — hs_clinical_indices
Continuum Removal — hs_continuum_removal
text: Removes spectral continuum (convex hull) spectrum. Useful enhancing absorption features normalizing baseline variations.

title: Continuum Removal — hs_continuum_removal
text: cube hsi_cube object. method Character. "division" (default) divides continuum, "subtraction" subtracts continuum.

title: Continuum Removal — hs_continuum_removal
text: hsi_cube object continuum-removed spectra.

title: Apply Dark Current Correction — hs_dark_correct
text: Subtracts dark reference cube data.

title: Apply Dark Current Correction — hs_dark_correct
text: cube hsi_cube object. dark hsi_cube object 3D array representing dark reference.

title: Apply Dark Current Correction — hs_dark_correct
text: hsi_cube object dark-corrected values.

title: Apply Dark Current Correction — hs_dark_correct
code: cube <- hs_simulate_cube(rows = 10, cols = 10, noise_sd = 0) dark <- hsi_cube(array(0.01, dim(cube$data)), cube$wavelengths) corrected <- hs_dark_correct(cube, dark)

title: Spectral Derivative — hs_derivative
text: Computes spectral derivative using Savitzky-Golay differentiation. Shorthand hs_smooth() deriv > 0.

title: Spectral Derivative — hs_derivative
text: cube hsi_cube object. order Integer. Derivative order. Default 1. window Integer (odd). SG window size. Default 5.

title: Spectral Derivative — hs_derivative
text: hsi_cube object derivative spectra.

what: Extract Endmember Spectra from a Cube — hs_endmembers
title: Extract Endmember Spectra from a Cube — hs_endmembers
text: Extracts endmember spectra specified pixel locations.

title: Extract Endmember Spectra from a Cube — hs_endmembers
Extract Endmember Spectra from a Cube — hs_endmembers
text: cube hsi_cube object. pixels Data.frame matrix columns x (col) y (row). labels Character vector class labels pixel. Default NULL (auto-generated).

title: Extract Endmember Spectra from a Cube — hs_endmembers
text: named matrix endmember spectra (rows = endmembers, cols = bands).

title: Extract Endmember Spectra from a Cube — hs_endmembers
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() pixels <- data.frame(x = c(5, 25), y = c(5, 25)) em <- hs_endmembers(cube, pixels, labels = c("region_1", "region_2")) dim(em) #> [1]  2 61

title: Get Example HSI Cube — hs_example_cube
text: Generates small pre-configured synthetic cube quick examples documentation. cube simulates 30x30 pixel tissue scene four regions varying oxygenation (healthy, ischemic, hyperemic, background).

title: Get Example HSI Cube — hs_example_cube
text: hsi_cube object (30 x 30 x 61 bands, 430-910 nm).

title: Get Example HSI Cube — hs_example_cube
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() print(cube) #>  #> ── hsi_cube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Dimensions: 30 rows x 30 cols x 61 bands #> Wavelengths: 430-910 nm (61 bands) #> FWHM: 25 nm (mean) #> Mask: 900/900 valid pixels (100%) #> Data range: [0.0198, 0.7306] #> Metadata: camera, processing_mode, acquisition_time, region_map, #> sto2_ground_truth, seed dim(cube) #> [1] 30 30 61

what: Export a Single-Band Image as PNG — hs_export_png
title: Export a Single-Band Image as PNG — hs_export_png
text: Exports single band index map HSI cube PNG image.

title: Export a Single-Band Image as PNG — hs_export_png
code: hs_export_png(   data,   path,   palette = "viridis",   range = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL )

title: Export a Single-Band Image as PNG — hs_export_png
text: data Numeric matrix export (rows x cols). path Character. Output file path (end .png). palette Character. Color palette: "viridis", "magma", "inferno", "plasma", "grey". Default "viridis". range Numeric vector length 2. Data range color mapping. Default NULL (auto data range). width Integer. Image width pixels. Default NULL (matches data cols). height Integer. Image height pixels. Default NULL (matches data rows).

title: Export a Single-Band Image as PNG — hs_export_png
text: Invisible path written file.

title: Export a Single-Band Image as PNG — hs_export_png
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() mat <- cube$data[, , 30] path <- file.path(tempdir(), "band30.png") hs_export_png(mat, path)

what: Detect and Correct Bad Pixels — hs_fix_bad_pixels
title: Detect and Correct Bad Pixels — hs_fix_bad_pixels
text: Identifies dead/hot pixels statistical deviation spatial neighborhood replaces neighborhood interpolation.

title: Detect and Correct Bad Pixels — hs_fix_bad_pixels
Detect and Correct Bad Pixels — hs_fix_bad_pixels
text: cube hsi_cube object. threshold Numeric. Standard deviation threshold detection. Default 3. method Character. Replacement method: "mean" "median" spatial neighbors. Default "median".

title: Detect and Correct Bad Pixels — hs_fix_bad_pixels
text: hsi_cube object corrected pixels.

title: Detect and Correct Bad Pixels — hs_fix_bad_pixels
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() # Introduce a hot pixel cube$data[15, 15, ] <- 999 fixed <- hs_fix_bad_pixels(cube, threshold = 3)

title: Minimum Noise Fraction Transform — hs_mnf
text: Orders components signal--noise ratio rather variance. Estimates noise covariance spatial first-differences.

title: Minimum Noise Fraction Transform — hs_mnf
text: cube hsi_cube object. n_components Integer. Number components. Default 5.

title: Minimum Noise Fraction Transform — hs_mnf
text: list class "hsi_mnf" (structure hs_pca()).

title: Minimum Noise Fraction Transform — hs_mnf
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() mnf <- hs_mnf(cube, n_components = 3) dim(mnf$scores) #> [1] 30 30  3

title: Multiplicative Scatter Correction — hs_msc
text: Corrects spectra multiplicative additive scatter effects regressing spectrum reference spectrum (default: mean spectrum).

title: Multiplicative Scatter Correction — hs_msc
text: cube hsi_cube object. reference Numeric vector. Reference spectrum. Default NULL = mean spectrum cube.

title: Multiplicative Scatter Correction — hs_msc
text: hsi_cube object MSC-corrected spectra.

what: Compute Normalized Difference Index (General Purpose) — hs_ndi
title: Compute Normalized Difference Index (General Purpose) — hs_ndi
text: NDI = (R_band1 - R_band2) / (R_band1 + R_band2). flexible building block two-band ratio index.

title: Compute Normalized Difference Index (General Purpose) — hs_ndi
Compute Normalized Difference Index (General Purpose) — hs_ndi
text: cube hsi_cube object. band1 Numeric. Center wavelength range c(min, max) first band. band2 Numeric. Center wavelength range c(min, max) second band.

title: Compute Normalized Difference Index (General Purpose) — hs_ndi
text: numeric matrix values [-1, 1].

title: Compute Normalized Difference Index (General Purpose) — hs_ndi
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() ndi <- hs_ndi(cube, band1 = 540, band2 = 660) range(ndi, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] -0.4915291 -0.2375021

title: Compute Near-Infrared Perfusion Index (NPI) — hs_npi
text: Estimates deeper tissue perfusion (4-6 mm depth) NIR wavelengths. Note: Cubert Ultris X MR upper limit 910 nm; original TIVITA NPI extends 925 nm. Results approximate Cubert data.

title: Compute Near-Infrared Perfusion Index (NPI) — hs_npi
text: cube hsi_cube object reflectance data. band1 Numeric vector length 2. Default c(655, 735). band2 Numeric vector length 2. Default c(825, 910).

title: Compute Near-Infrared Perfusion Index (NPI) — hs_npi
text: numeric matrix values 0-100.

title: PCA on Hyperspectral Cube — hs_pca
text: Performs Principal Component Analysis spectral dimension HSI cube. Reduces spectral bands smaller number orthogonal components ordered explained variance.

title: PCA on Hyperspectral Cube — hs_pca
code: hs_pca(cube, n_components = 5L, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

title: PCA on Hyperspectral Cube — hs_pca
text: cube hsi_cube object. n_components Integer. Number components retain. Default 5. center Logical. Center bands PCA. Default TRUE. scale Logical. Scale bands unit variance. Default FALSE.

title: PCA on Hyperspectral Cube — hs_pca
text: list class "hsi_pca": scores 3D array (rows x cols x n_components) component score maps. loadings Matrix (bands x n_components) spectral loadings. variance_explained Numeric vector proportion variance per component. center Centering vector used (FALSE). scale Scaling vector used (FALSE). wavelengths Wavelength vector input cube.

title: PCA on Hyperspectral Cube — hs_pca
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() pca <- hs_pca(cube, n_components = 3) pca$variance_explained #> [1] 0.723528996 0.008620008 0.006954791

title: TIVITA-Style Clinical Panel Display — hs_plot_clinical
text: Generates side--side panel display RGB image plus clinical tissue indices (StO2, NPI, THI, TWI), matching established surgical HSI visualization paradigm.

title: TIVITA-Style Clinical Panel Display — hs_plot_clinical
code: hs_plot_clinical(   cube,   indices = c("sto2", "npi", "thi"),   mask_background = TRUE,   threshold = 0.05,   ncol = NULL )

title: TIVITA-Style Clinical Panel Display — hs_plot_clinical
text: cube hsi_cube object reflectance data. indices Character vector. indices display. Default c("sto2", "npi", "thi"). TWI included wavelengths permit. mask_background Logical. Mask non-tissue pixels. Default TRUE. threshold Numeric. Masking threshold mean reflectance. Default 0.05. ncol Integer. Number panel columns. Default NULL (auto).

title: TIVITA-Style Clinical Panel Display — hs_plot_clinical
text: patchwork composite ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Plot Single-Band Spatial Image — hs_plot_image
text: Displays single spectral band spatial image using pseudocolor palette.

title: Plot Single-Band Spatial Image — hs_plot_image
code: hs_plot_image(cube, wavelength = NULL, band = NULL, palette = "viridis")

title: Plot Single-Band Spatial Image — hs_plot_image
text: cube hsi_cube object. wavelength Numeric. Center wavelength display (nearest band selected). Default NULL. band Integer. Band index. Alternative wavelength. Default NULL. palette Character. Color palette name. Default "viridis".

title: Plot Single-Band Spatial Image — hs_plot_image
text: ggplot2::ggplot object using ggplot2::geom_raster().

what: Plot Index Map with Clinical Color Scale — hs_plot_index
title: Plot Index Map with Clinical Color Scale — hs_plot_index
text: Displays single tissue index pseudocolor spatial map clinically meaningful color scales.

title: Plot Index Map with Clinical Color Scale — hs_plot_index
code: hs_plot_index(   index_matrix,   title = "",   palette = "sto2",   range = c(0, 100),   mask = NULL )

title: Plot Index Map with Clinical Color Scale — hs_plot_index
text: index_matrix Numeric matrix (rows x cols) index function. title Character. Map title (e.g., "StO2 (%)"). Default "". palette Character. "sto2" (blue-red diverging), "perfusion" (viridis), "hemoglobin" (magma), "water" (mako). Default "sto2". range Numeric vector length 2. Display range. Default c(0, 100). mask Logical matrix. Pixels mask (FALSE = masked). Default NULL.

title: Plot Index Map with Clinical Color Scale — hs_plot_index
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Plot Index Map with Clinical Color Scale — hs_plot_index
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() sto2 <- hs_sto2(cube) hs_plot_index(sto2, title = "StO2 (%)", palette = "sto2")

what: Synthesize RGB Image from Spectral Cube — hs_plot_rgb
title: Synthesize RGB Image from Spectral Cube — hs_plot_rgb
text: Creates pseudo-color RGB composite mapping three wavelength bands red, green, blue channels.

title: Synthesize RGB Image from Spectral Cube — hs_plot_rgb
Synthesize RGB Image from Spectral Cube — hs_plot_rgb
text: cube hsi_cube object. r, g, b Numeric. Center wavelengths R, G, B channels. Defaults: r=640, g=550, b=460. stretch Character. Histogram stretch: "linear" (default), "none".

title: Synthesize RGB Image from Spectral Cube — hs_plot_rgb
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Synthesize RGB Image from Spectral Cube — hs_plot_rgb
Plot Spectral Profiles — hs_plot_spectra
text: Displays spectral profiles HSI cube. Can show mean spectrum, random pixel spectra, spectra specific pixel locations.

title: Plot Spectral Profiles — hs_plot_spectra
text: cube hsi_cube object. pixels Character data.frame. "mean" spatial mean, "random" random pixel sample, data.frame columns x y. Default "mean". n Integer. Number random spectra pixels = "random". Default 100. show_sd Logical. Show mean +/- SD ribbon. Default TRUE pixels = "mean".

title: Plot Spectral Profiles — hs_plot_spectra
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

what: Read a Hyperspectral Data Cube from Any Supported Format — hs_read_cube
title: Read a Hyperspectral Data Cube from Any Supported Format — hs_read_cube
text: Auto-detects format file extension dispatches appropriate reader. Supported formats: ENVI (.hdr), TIFF (.tif/.tiff), Cubert (.cu3s).

title: Read a Hyperspectral Data Cube from Any Supported Format — hs_read_cube
Read a Hyperspectral Data Cube from Any Supported Format — hs_read_cube
text: path Path hyperspectral data file. ... Additional arguments passed format-specific reader.

title: Read a Hyperspectral Data Cube from Any Supported Format — hs_read_cube
text: hsi_cube object.

title: Read a Hyperspectral Data Cube from Any Supported Format — hs_read_cube
code: hdr_path <- hs_example_files() cube <- hs_read_cube(hdr_path, verbose = FALSE) dim(cube) #> [1] 10 10 11

what: Read a Cubert .cu3s Session File — hs_read_cubert
title: Read a Cubert .cu3s Session File — hs_read_cubert
text: Reads Cubert session files using cuvis.r package, R wrapper Cubert CUVIS C SDK (see https://github.com/r-heller/cuvis.r).

title: Read a Cubert .cu3s Session File — hs_read_cubert
code: hs_read_cubert(   path,   index = 1L,   mode = c("reflectance", "spectral_radiance", "dark_subtract", "raw"),   settings_dir = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Read a Cubert .cu3s Session File — hs_read_cubert
text: path Path .cu3s Cubert session file. index Integer. Measurement index within session (1-based). Default 1. mode Character. Processing mode: "reflectance" (default), "spectral_radiance", "dark_subtract", "raw". settings_dir Character NULL. Path CUVIS settings directory (e.g., "C:/ProgramData/cuvis"). NULL (default), uses CUVIS_SETTINGS environment variable temporary directory. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Read a Cubert .cu3s Session File — hs_read_cubert
text: hsi_cube object. Reflectance data 0-1 range.

title: Read a Cubert .cu3s Session File — hs_read_cubert
text: processing context automatically loads dark/white references embedded session file, separate reference files typically needed. Cubert reflectance values stored uint16 scaled 10000 (.e. 10000 = 100% reflectance). function automatically converts fractional reflectance (0-1) mode = "reflectance".

title: Read a Cubert .cu3s Session File — hs_read_cubert
code: # \donttest{ # Requires cuvis.r package and Cubert CUVIS SDK # cube <- hs_read_cubert("path/to/session.cu3s") # }

what: Read an ENVI Hyperspectral Image File — hs_read_envi
title: Read an ENVI Hyperspectral Image File — hs_read_envi
text: Reads ENVI format hyperspectral data consisting .hdr header file binary data file. Supports BSQ, BIL, BIP interleave formats.

title: Read an ENVI Hyperspectral Image File — hs_read_envi
code: hs_read_envi(   path,   backend = "auto",   bands = NULL,   extent = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Read an ENVI Hyperspectral Image File — hs_read_envi
text: path Path ENVI header file (.hdr) binary file. backend Character. "auto" (default) uses terra available, otherwise falls back built-reader. "builtin" forces pure-R reader. "terra" forces terra (errors installed). bands Integer vector band indices read. Default NULL = . extent Numeric vector c(row_start, row_end, col_start, col_end) spatial subset. Default NULL = full image. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Read an ENVI Hyperspectral Image File — hs_read_envi
text: hsi_cube object.

title: Read an ENVI Hyperspectral Image File — hs_read_envi
code: hdr_path <- hs_example_files() cube <- hs_read_envi(hdr_path, verbose = FALSE) dim(cube) #> [1] 10 10 11

what: Read a Multi-Channel TIFF File — hs_read_tiff
title: Read a Multi-Channel TIFF File — hs_read_tiff
text: Reads multi-band TIFF file hyperspectral cube. Requires terra package TIFF reading.

title: Read a Multi-Channel TIFF File — hs_read_tiff
code: hs_read_tiff(path, wavelengths, fwhm = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

title: Read a Multi-Channel TIFF File — hs_read_tiff
text: path Path multi-channel TIFF file. wavelengths Numeric vector wavelengths (required TIFF files lack spectral metadata). fwhm Numeric vector FWHM values. Default NULL. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Read a Multi-Channel TIFF File — hs_read_tiff
text: hsi_cube object.

title: Read a Multi-Channel TIFF File — hs_read_tiff
code: # \donttest{ # Requires terra package # cube <- hs_read_tiff("path/to/image.tif", wavelengths = seq(430, 910, by = 8)) # }

what: Resample Spectra to New Wavelength Grid — hs_resample
title: Resample Spectra to New Wavelength Grid — hs_resample
text: Interpolates spectral data new set wavelength positions.

title: Resample Spectra to New Wavelength Grid — hs_resample
Resample Spectra to New Wavelength Grid — hs_resample
text: cube hsi_cube object. target_wavelengths Numeric vector. Target wavelength positions nm. method Character. Interpolation method: "linear" (default), "spline".

title: Resample Spectra to New Wavelength Grid — hs_resample
text: hsi_cube object resampled spectra.

title: Resample Spectra to New Wavelength Grid — hs_resample
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() resampled <- hs_resample(cube, target_wavelengths = seq(450, 900, by = 10)) length(resampled$wavelengths) #> [1] 46

title: Define Polygon ROI — hs_roi_polygon
text: Creates polygon region interest using point--polygon testing.

title: Define Polygon ROI — hs_roi_polygon
text: cube hsi_cube object. vertices Matrix columns x y (polygon vertices pixel coordinates).

title: Define Polygon ROI — hs_roi_polygon
text: list class "hsi_roi" containing mask.

title: Define Polygon ROI — hs_roi_polygon
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() verts <- matrix(c(5, 5, 15, 15, 5, 20, 5, 20), ncol = 2,                 dimnames = list(NULL, c("x", "y"))) roi <- hs_roi_polygon(cube, verts)

title: Define Rectangular ROI — hs_roi_rect
text: Creates rectangular region interest HSI cube.

title: Define Rectangular ROI — hs_roi_rect
text: cube hsi_cube object. x_range Numeric vector length 2 (column indices, min max). y_range Numeric vector length 2 (row indices, min max).

title: Define Rectangular ROI — hs_roi_rect
text: list class "hsi_roi" containing mask bounds.

title: Define Rectangular ROI — hs_roi_rect
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() roi <- hs_roi_rect(cube, x_range = c(5, 15), y_range = c(5, 15)) sum(roi$mask) #> [1] 121

title: Compute ROI Statistics — hs_roi_stats
text: Computes per-band spectral statistics within region interest.

title: Compute ROI Statistics — hs_roi_stats
text: cube hsi_cube object. roi hsi_roi object (hs_roi_rect() hs_roi_polygon()), logical mask matrix.

title: Compute ROI Statistics — hs_roi_stats
text: tibble::tibble columns wavelength, mean, sd, median, min, max, n_pixels.

what: Launch Interactive Hyperspectral Image Explorer — hs_run_app
title: Launch Interactive Hyperspectral Image Explorer — hs_run_app
text: Opens Shiny application interactive exploration hyperspectral cubes. Provides band selection, spectral profiling, clinical index mapping, preprocessing, classification, export tools across six tabs.

title: Launch Interactive Hyperspectral Image Explorer — hs_run_app
code: hs_run_app(cube = NULL, port = NULL, launch.browser = TRUE)

title: Launch Interactive Hyperspectral Image Explorer — hs_run_app
text: cube hsi_cube object explore. NULL (default), app starts example cube. port Integer. Port Shiny server. Default NULL (auto). launch.browser Logical. Open browser. Default TRUE.

title: Launch Interactive Hyperspectral Image Explorer — hs_run_app
text: Invisible NULL. Launches Shiny application.

title: Launch Interactive Hyperspectral Image Explorer — hs_run_app
Spectral Angle Mapper Classification — hs_sam
text: Classifies pixel computing spectral angle set reference endmember spectra assigning class smallest angle.

title: Spectral Angle Mapper Classification — hs_sam
text: cube hsi_cube object. endmembers Named matrix data.frame. Rows = endmembers, cols = bands. Row names become class labels. threshold Numeric. Maximum angle (radians) assignment. Pixels exceeding classified "unclassified". Default 0.1.

title: Spectral Angle Mapper Classification — hs_sam
text: list class "hsi_classification": class_map Character matrix (rows x cols) class labels. angle_map 3D array angles endmember. endmembers endmember matrix used.

title: Spectral Angle Mapper Classification — hs_sam
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() # Define endmembers from specific pixels em <- rbind(   healthy = cube$data[5, 5, ],   ischemic = cube$data[25, 5, ] ) result <- hs_sam(cube, em, threshold = 0.5) table(result$class_map) #>  #>  healthy ischemic  #>      407      493

what: Generate a Synthetic HSI Cube for Testing and Examples — hs_simulate_cube
title: Generate a Synthetic HSI Cube for Testing and Examples — hs_simulate_cube
text: Creates realistic synthetic tissue hyperspectral cube known ground truth. Simulates tissue scene regions varying oxygenation, hemoglobin concentration, background (non-tissue) region.

title: Generate a Synthetic HSI Cube for Testing and Examples — hs_simulate_cube
code: hs_simulate_cube(   rows = 50L,   cols = 50L,   wavelengths = seq(430, 910, by = 8),   n_regions = 4L,   sto2_range = c(0.3, 0.95),   noise_sd = 0.01,   seed = 42L )

title: Generate a Synthetic HSI Cube for Testing and Examples — hs_simulate_cube
text: rows Integer. Spatial rows. Default 50. cols Integer. Spatial columns. Default 50. wavelengths Numeric vector. Wavelength grid nm. Default: seq(430, 910, = 8) matching Cubert Ultris X MR. n_regions Integer. Number distinct tissue regions. Default 4. sto2_range Numeric vector length 2. Range StO2 values (0 1). Default c(0.3, 0.95). noise_sd Numeric. Gaussian noise standard deviation. Default 0.01. seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. Default 42.

title: Generate a Synthetic HSI Cube for Testing and Examples — hs_simulate_cube
text: hsi_cube object metadata including ground-truth region_map.

title: Generate a Synthetic HSI Cube for Testing and Examples — hs_simulate_cube
Savitzky-Golay Spectral Smoothing — hs_smooth
text: Applies Savitzky-Golay filter along spectral dimension. Optionally computes spectral derivatives. Uses prospectr signal available, otherwise falls back built-convolution implementation.

title: Savitzky-Golay Spectral Smoothing — hs_smooth
text: cube hsi_cube object. window Integer (odd). Filter window size bands. Default 5. poly Integer. Polynomial order. Must less window. Default 2. deriv Integer. Derivative order (0 = smoothing , 1 = first derivative, 2 = second derivative). Default 0.

title: Savitzky-Golay Spectral Smoothing — hs_smooth
text: hsi_cube object smoothed/differentiated spectra.

title: Savitzky-Golay Spectral Smoothing — hs_smooth
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() smoothed <- hs_smooth(cube, window = 5, poly = 2) dim(smoothed) #> [1] 30 30 61

title: Standard Normal Variate Correction — hs_snv
text: Normalizes spectrum zero mean unit variance. Useful reducing multiplicative scatter effects baseline variation.

title: Standard Normal Variate Correction — hs_snv
text: cube hsi_cube object.

title: Standard Normal Variate Correction — hs_snv
text: hsi_cube object SNV-corrected spectra.

title: Compute Tissue Oxygen Saturation (StO2) — hs_sto2
text: Estimates superficial tissue oxygenation visible-range hemoglobin absorption. Uses ratio reflectance 500-650 nm (oxy/deoxy-Hb Q-bands) 700-815 nm (NIR oxy-Hb shoulder) regions.

title: Compute Tissue Oxygen Saturation (StO2) — hs_sto2
code: hs_sto2(cube, band1 = c(500, 650), band2 = c(700, 815), method = "ratio")

title: Compute Tissue Oxygen Saturation (StO2) — hs_sto2
text: cube hsi_cube object reflectance data. band1 Numeric vector length 2. Wavelength range first band (default c(500, 650), visible Hb absorption). band2 Numeric vector length 2. Wavelength range second band (default c(700, 815), NIR region). method Character. "ratio" band-ratio index (default), "beer_lambert" full chromophore fitting.

title: Compute Tissue Oxygen Saturation (StO2) — hs_sto2
text: numeric matrix (rows x cols) values 0-100 representing estimated tissue oxygen saturation percentage. Returns NA masked pixels.

title: Compute Tissue Oxygen Saturation (StO2) — hs_sto2
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() sto2 <- hs_sto2(cube) range(sto2, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1]   0 100

title: Compute Tissue Hemoglobin Index (THI) — hs_thi
text: Estimates relative hemoglobin concentration superficial depth.

title: Compute Tissue Hemoglobin Index (THI) — hs_thi
text: cube hsi_cube object. band1 Numeric vector length 2. Default c(530, 590) (Hb Q-bands). band2 Numeric vector length 2. Default c(785, 825) (reference).

title: Compute Tissue Hemoglobin Index (THI) — hs_thi
text: numeric matrix values 0-100.

title: Compute Tissue Water Index (TWI) — hs_twi
text: Estimates tissue water content 960 nm water absorption band. Cubert Ultris X MR (430-910 nm) fully cover range.

title: Compute Tissue Water Index (TWI) — hs_twi
text: cube hsi_cube object. numerator Numeric vector length 2. Default c(880, 910) (adapted Cubert range). denominator Numeric vector length 2. Default c(830, 870).

title: Compute Tissue Water Index (TWI) — hs_twi
text: numeric matrix values 0-100, NA matrix warning required wavelengths unavailable.

what: UMAP Embedding of Spectral Data — hs_umap
title: UMAP Embedding of Spectral Data — hs_umap
text: Computes UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation Projection) embedding spectral data. Requires uwot package.

title: UMAP Embedding of Spectral Data — hs_umap
code: hs_umap(   cube,   n_components = 2L,   n_neighbors = 15L,   min_dist = 0.1,   pca_pre = 20L )

title: UMAP Embedding of Spectral Data — hs_umap
text: cube hsi_cube object. n_components Integer. UMAP dimensions. Default 2. n_neighbors Integer. Default 15. min_dist Numeric. Default 0.1. pca_pre Integer NULL. PCA pre-reduction dimensionality. Default 20.

title: UMAP Embedding of Spectral Data — hs_umap
text: list class "hsi_umap" containing embedding matrix spatial coordinates.

title: UMAP Embedding of Spectral Data — hs_umap
code: # \donttest{ # Requires uwot package cube <- hs_example_cube() if (requireNamespace("uwot", quietly = TRUE)) {   umap_result <- hs_umap(cube, n_components = 2) } # }

what: Linear Spectral Unmixing via NNLS — hs_unmix_nnls
title: Linear Spectral Unmixing via NNLS — hs_unmix_nnls
text: Solves linear mixing model per pixel using non-negative least squares.

title: Linear Spectral Unmixing via NNLS — hs_unmix_nnls
Linear Spectral Unmixing via NNLS — hs_unmix_nnls
text: cube hsi_cube object (reflectance absorbance). endmembers Numeric matrix. Columns = endmember spectra. Rows = bands. Column names become abundance map labels. sum_to_one Logical. Apply sum--one constraint. Default FALSE.

title: Linear Spectral Unmixing via NNLS — hs_unmix_nnls
text: list class "hsi_unmix": abundances 3D array (rows x cols x n_endmembers) abundance maps. residuals 3D array reconstruction residuals. rmse Numeric matrix per-pixel RMSE. endmember_names Character vector.

title: Linear Spectral Unmixing via NNLS — hs_unmix_nnls
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() # Create simple endmembers em <- cbind(   tissue = cube$data[5, 5, ],   background = cube$data[25, 25, ] ) result <- hs_unmix_nnls(cube, em) dim(result$abundances) #> [1] 30 30  2

title: Apply White Reference Normalization — hs_white_normalize
text: Normalizes cube white reference, optionally dark correction.

title: Apply White Reference Normalization — hs_white_normalize
text: cube hsi_cube object. white hsi_cube object 3D array representing white reference. dark hsi_cube object 3D array representing dark reference. Default NULL (dark subtraction).

title: Apply White Reference Normalization — hs_white_normalize
text: hsi_cube object normalized values.

title: Apply White Reference Normalization — hs_white_normalize
code: cube <- hs_simulate_cube(rows = 10, cols = 10, noise_sd = 0) white <- hsi_cube(array(0.95, dim(cube$data)), cube$wavelengths) norm <- hs_white_normalize(cube, white)

what: Write an HSI Cube to ENVI Format — hs_write_envi
title: Write an HSI Cube to ENVI Format — hs_write_envi
text: Writes hsi_cube object ENVI header (.hdr) binary (.dat) file pair.

title: Write an HSI Cube to ENVI Format — hs_write_envi
code: hs_write_envi(cube, path, interleave = "bsq", data_type = 4L, verbose = TRUE)

title: Write an HSI Cube to ENVI Format — hs_write_envi
text: cube hsi_cube object. path Character. Output path without extension. Files .hdr .dat created. interleave Character. Data interleave format: "bsq" (default), "bil", "bip". data_type Integer. ENVI data type code. Default 4 (float32). verbose Logical. Print progress. Default TRUE.

title: Write an HSI Cube to ENVI Format — hs_write_envi
text: Invisible character vector written file paths.

title: Write an HSI Cube to ENVI Format — hs_write_envi
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() dir <- tempdir() paths <- hs_write_envi(cube, file.path(dir, "test_cube"), verbose = FALSE) file.exists(paths) #> [1] TRUE TRUE

Write an HSI Cube to Multi-Band TIFF — hs_write_tiff
text: cube hsi_cube object. path Character. Output path (end .tif). verbose Logical. Print progress. Default TRUE.

text: Invisible path written file.

title: Write an HSI Cube to Multi-Band TIFF — hs_write_tiff

what: Create an HSI Cube Object — hsi_cube
title: Create an HSI Cube Object — hsi_cube
text: Constructs hyperspectral imaging data cube S3 object. cube stores 3D array spectral data (rows x columns x bands) along wavelength metadata, spatial mask, additional metadata.

title: Create an HSI Cube Object — hsi_cube
code: hsi_cube(data, wavelengths, fwhm = NULL, metadata = list(), mask = NULL)

title: Create an HSI Cube Object — hsi_cube
text: data Numeric 3D array dimensions (rows, cols, bands). wavelengths Numeric vector band center wavelengths nanometers. Length must match dim(data)[3]. fwhm Numeric vector full-width--half-maximum values per band nm. Default NULL (unknown). scalar, recycled bands. metadata Named list metadata (camera model, integration time, processing mode, acquisition timestamp, etc.). Default empty list. mask Logical matrix matching spatial dimensions. TRUE = valid pixel. Default NULL (pixels valid).

title: Create an HSI Cube Object — hsi_cube
text: hsi_cube S3 object (named list class "hsi_cube").

title: Create an HSI Cube Object — hsi_cube
code: # Create a small synthetic cube data <- array(runif(10 * 10 * 5), dim = c(10, 10, 5)) wavelengths <- c(500, 550, 600, 650, 700) cube <- hsi_cube(data, wavelengths) print(cube) #>  #> ── hsi_cube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Dimensions: 10 rows x 10 cols x 5 bands #> Wavelengths: 500-700 nm (5 bands) #> Data range: [0.005, 0.9999]

what: hyperspectR: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications — hyperspectR-package
title: hyperspectR: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications — hyperspectR-package
text: Read, process, analyze, visualize hyperspectral imaging (HSI) data Cubert snapshot cameras standard ENVI/TIFF formats. Provides calibration workflows (dark correction, white reference normalization), spectral preprocessing (Savitzky-Golay smoothing, SNV, MSC, derivatives), biomedical tissue indices (tissue oxygenation StO2, perfusion NPI, hemoglobin THI, water content TWI), dimensionality reduction (PCA, MNF), pixel-wise classification (SAM, SVM, random forest), spectral unmixing via Beer-Lambert chromophore fitting. Includes interactive 'shiny' application clinical hyperspectral image exploration. Designed intraoperative imaging, soft tissue diagnostics, perfusion monitoring workflows.

title: hyperspectR: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Biomedical Applications — hyperspectR-package
text: Maintainer: Raban Heller raban.heller@charite.de (ORCID)

title: Print an hsi_cube Object — print.hsi_cube
text: Displays compact summary hyperspectral cube.

title: Print an hsi_cube Object — print.hsi_cube
text: x hsi_cube object. ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Print an hsi_cube Object — print.hsi_cube
text: Invisible x.

title: Print an hsi_cube Object — print.hsi_cube
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() print(cube) #>  #> ── hsi_cube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Dimensions: 30 rows x 30 cols x 61 bands #> Wavelengths: 430-910 nm (61 bands) #> FWHM: 25 nm (mean) #> Mask: 900/900 valid pixels (100%) #> Data range: [0.0198, 0.7306] #> Metadata: camera, processing_mode, acquisition_time, region_map, #> sto2_ground_truth, seed

what: Wavelength-to-Color Scale for Spectral Plots — scale_color_wavelength
title: Wavelength-to-Color Scale for Spectral Plots — scale_color_wavelength
text: Maps wavelength values approximate visible light colors spectral line coloring. Returns ggplot2 continuous color scale.

title: Wavelength-to-Color Scale for Spectral Plots — scale_color_wavelength
Wavelength-to-Color Scale for Spectral Plots — scale_color_wavelength
text: ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn().

title: Wavelength-to-Color Scale for Spectral Plots — scale_color_wavelength
text: ggplot2::Scale object.

title: Wavelength-to-Color Scale for Spectral Plots — scale_color_wavelength
code: cube <- hs_example_cube() library(ggplot2) df <- data.frame(   wavelength = cube$wavelengths,   value = cube$data[15, 15, ] ) ggplot(df, aes(wavelength, value, color = wavelength)) +   geom_line() +   scale_color_wavelength()

title: Summarize an hsi_cube Object — summary.hsi_cube
text: Returns named list cube statistics including per-band means standard deviations.

title: Summarize an hsi_cube Object — summary.hsi_cube
text: object hsi_cube object. ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Summarize an hsi_cube Object — summary.hsi_cube
text: named list elements dimensions, wavelength_range, n_bands, data_range, band_means, band_sds, n_valid_pixels, metadata.

title: Minimalist HSI Theme — theme_hsi
text: clean ggplot2 theme designed hyperspectral image display. Uses minimal styling white background.

title: Minimalist HSI Theme — theme_hsi
text: base_size Numeric. Base font size. Default 11.

title: Minimalist HSI Theme — theme_hsi
text: ggplot2::theme object.

title: Minimalist HSI Theme — theme_hsi
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(data.frame(x = 1:10, y = 1:10), aes(x, y)) +   geom_point() +   theme_hsi()

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: hs_read_envi(): Read ENVI .hdr + binary hyperspectral cubes (BSQ/BIL/BIP). hs_read_tiff(): Read multi-channel TIFF files. hs_read_cubert(): Read Cubert .cu3s session files via Python bridge (optional). hs_read_cube(): Auto-detect format dispatch correct reader. hs_write_envi(), hs_write_tiff(), hs_export_png(): Export functions.

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: hs_calibrate(): Full calibration workflow (dark + white reference). hs_fix_bad_pixels(): Statistical bad pixel detection interpolation.

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: hs_smooth(): Savitzky-Golay smoothing derivatives. hs_snv(): Standard Normal Variate correction. hs_msc(): Multiplicative Scatter Correction. hs_absorbance(): Reflectance absorbance conversion. hs_continuum_removal(), hs_resample().

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: hs_sto2(): Tissue oxygen saturation (500-815 nm). hs_npi(): Near-infrared perfusion index (655-910 nm). hs_thi(): Tissue hemoglobin index (530-825 nm). hs_twi(): Tissue water index (adapted Cubert range). hs_ndi(): Generic normalized difference index. hs_clinical_indices(): Compute available indices.

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: hs_pca(), hs_mnf(): Dimensionality reduction. hs_umap(): UMAP spectral embedding (optional, requires uwot). hs_sam(): Spectral Angle Mapper classification. hs_classify_svm(), hs_classify_rf(): Machine learning classification. hs_unmix_nnls(): Linear spectral unmixing via NNLS. hs_beer_lambert(): Chromophore concentration fitting.

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: autoplot.hsi_cube(): Plot RGB, single band, spectra. hs_plot_clinical(): TIVITA-style clinical panel display. hs_plot_index(): Pseudocolor index map clinical palette. hs_plot_spectra(): Spectral profile plots mean +/- SD. scale_color_wavelength(), theme_hsi().

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: hs_run_app(): Interactive 6-tab HSI explorer.

title: hyperspectR 0.1.0
text: hs_example_cube(): Synthetic 30x30 tissue cube (430-910 nm, 61 bands). hs_simulate_cube(): Configurable synthetic cube generator. hs_chromophore_data(): Published HbO2/Hb extinction coefficients.
